mainQuestTheme: Receiving direction, guidance, and timing without forcing the path, overcorrecting the flow, or losing trust in the body's orientation.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The path is not visible yet, but the body can feel where not to go.

questBrief.mission: Trust the first directional signal before the whole path is visible.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Notice what your body is already steering away from.
questBrief.livePrompt: What direction feels wrong before you know why?

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This quest begins with orientation before movement. The body senses direction through subtle yes and no signals before the mind can explain the route.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: Trying to know the whole path too early overrides the first true guidance. Direction starts by respecting what does not feel correct.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You keep asking for the map because the first no feels too quiet.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you ignoring a quiet refusal because it lacks explanation?

assetsAndFriction.collapsedPreview: The body knows before the route.
assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You treat uncertainty like the absence of guidance.
frictionSignals: demanding the full path before respecting the first signal
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where uncertainty makes you abandon orientation.

groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body settles into the direction that feels clean before the mind builds a route.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Do your feet want to move, pause, or turn away?

unlockCondition.questKey: Feel the first refusal → stop forward motion → wait until the next direction appears
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels more stable when it stops forcing movement.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Name what feels incorrect before deciding what comes next.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: A quiet no is still guidance.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The right direction opens when you stop trying to manufacture it.

questBrief.mission: Receive the path that is already opening instead of correcting it into a plan.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Notice where the next step feels available without effort.

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line receives direction naturally. The movement is not forced; the path reveals itself through alignment, timing, and an ease that does not need to be justified.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: The gift is being carried by the right direction. The risk is interfering because ease feels too simple to trust.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You start fixing the path right when it was already carrying you.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you improving what is already aligned?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You complicate alignment to feel in control.
frictionSignals: adding strategy where response is enough
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where control enters after ease appears.

groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body receives direction without bracing or steering too hard.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Does your body feel carried, or are you trying to drive?

unlockCondition.questKey: Notice the open path → take the next step → do not add control until reality asks
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels supported without needing to force momentum.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Take the obvious next step before designing the whole route.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Let alignment move before strategy interrupts.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The wrong turns teach the body what alignment actually feels like.

questBrief.mission: Use wrong turns as feedback instead of proof that guidance failed.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Track which movement made the body lose orientation.
questBrief.livePrompt: What wrong turn taught you something?

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line learns direction through movement, mistake, detour, and correction. The body discovers what alignment is by feeling what throws it off.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: A wrong turn becomes useful when it changes the next step. It becomes a loop when the same misalignment keeps being repeated.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You keep calling it exploration after your body already knows it is a detour.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you repeating a wrong turn as if it is still discovery?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You confuse wandering with being guided.
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where movement keeps avoiding alignment.

groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body registers misalignment instead of rushing into the next direction.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Does your stomach settle here, or signal that the path is off?

unlockCondition.questKey: Take the step → feel the mismatch → change direction before repeating it
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels clearer after admitting the path is off.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Name what the detour showed before moving again.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: Exploration becomes avoidance when it ignores feedback.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Let the wrong turn update the route.

mainQuestContent.title: Directional Support

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The path becomes clearer when direction is held with others.

questBrief.mission: Share orientation without forcing others to follow your path.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Find where guidance needs to become shared language.
questBrief.livePrompt: Who needs the path made visible?

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line works through guidance, support, and relational orientation. The path becomes clearer when direction can be communicated, held, and responded to by others.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: Private direction may be correct, but shared movement needs language. Guidance must invite response without becoming control.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You know the direction, then get frustrated that no one can follow what you never made clear.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you expecting others to understand an unspoken route?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You turn orientation into quiet expectation.
frictionSignals: expecting others to follow without explanation
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where support becomes silent pressure.

groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body stays receptive while making direction visible.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Can your chest stay open while others respond differently?

unlockCondition.questKey: Name the direction → make the next step visible → let others choose their response
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels clear without needing to control the whole field.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Say the next step plainly instead of implying it.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: Guidance lands cleaner when it leaves room for response.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Make the route visible without forcing the movement.

driftPattern: expecting others to follow unspoken direction

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: Others project the path onto you before you have chosen it.

questBrief.mission: Separate true guidance from the path others want you to carry.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Notice where others are treating your direction as their answer.
questBrief.livePrompt: Who is projecting the path onto you?

fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line can be seen as directional, guiding, or quietly certain. Others may project leadership, purpose, or a path onto you before your own orientation is fully clear.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: Projection can turn receptivity into performance. The task is to protect true direction from becoming someone else's expectation.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You start walking the path because they already believed it was yours.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you following a direction built from their expectation?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You become the path others need to see.
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where being trusted turns into being assigned.

groundingProtocol: relax the back of the neck
groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body returns to its own orientation instead of rising into the projected role.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Are you following your direction, or wearing theirs?

unlockCondition.questKey: Notice the projection → return to body orientation → decline any path that is not yours
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels centered when the path is not borrowed.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Say what is actually clear before accepting any role.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: Being seen as directional does not mean the path is yours.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Let projection fall away before you move.

hero.atmosphericSubtitle: The deepest guidance may ask for stillness before motion.

questBrief.mission: Discern when the correct path is movement, waiting, refusal, or surrender.
questBrief.fieldPrompt: Notice where not moving is the aligned response.

fieldBriefing.collapsedPreview: Do not mistake stillness for failure.
fieldBriefing.whatThisQuestIsAbout: This line sees direction from a wider distance. Sometimes guidance arrives as movement, and sometimes it arrives as waiting, releasing, or refusing to move before the path is ready.
fieldBriefing.whyThisMattersNow: Forcing movement can break alignment. Mature receptivity knows that not moving can be an active form of guidance.
fieldBriefing.primaryRecognitionTrigger: You call it stuck because the right path has not given you movement yet.
fieldBriefing.livePrompt: Where are you judging stillness as failure?

assetsAndFriction.questTrap: You force motion to avoid surrender.
assetsAndFriction.livePrompt: Watch where fear of stillness creates false movement.

groundingProtocol: lengthen the back of the neck
groundingEffect.somaticShift: The body can remain oriented without needing immediate movement.
groundingEffect.bodyCheckPrompt: Can your spine stay steady while the path remains still?

unlockCondition.questKey: Feel the stillness → confirm no clean movement → hold orientation until timing opens
unlockCondition.embodiedSignal: The body feels receptive, steady, and unforced.
unlockCondition.ritualPrompt: Name what is not ready before choosing motion.
unlockCondition.alignmentReminder: Stillness can be guidance, not absence.
unlockCondition.livePrompt: Let the path open before you move.

perkTrees.subtitle: Develop the capacities behind Foundation of Direction.

nodes.name: Directional Receptivity
nodes.description: This path develops the ability to receive direction through foundation of direction.
nodes.effect: Guidance becomes usable when the body can orient without forcing the path.
nodes.developmentCue: Notice whether direction is arriving through movement, refusal, support, projection, or stillness.
nodes.masteryMarker: You can follow guidance without manufacturing certainty.

nodes.name: Directional Sensitivity
nodes.description: You learn to recognize directional signals before the full route is visible.
nodes.effect: The first true yes, no, pause, or turn becomes easier to trust.
nodes.developmentCue: Track what the body knows before the mind explains it.
nodes.masteryMarker: You can separate real orientation from mental map-making.

nodes.name: Body-Led Refusal
nodes.description: The body learns to stay receptive while direction is still forming.
nodes.effect: Movement comes from grounded orientation instead of urgency.
nodes.developmentCue: Let the body settle before choosing the path.
nodes.masteryMarker: You remain physically steady without needing the whole route.

nodes.name: Orientation Patience
nodes.description: The path becomes clearer when direction is held in relationship with timing and response.
nodes.effect: Guidance can move through the field without turning into control.
nodes.developmentCue: Watch how the path opens, closes, redirects, or waits.
nodes.masteryMarker: You can support direction without forcing movement.

nodes.name: Clean First Step
nodes.description: The full path matures into receptivity that trusts timing without collapsing into passivity.
nodes.effect: Direction becomes clean because it is received, confirmed, and followed without distortion.
nodes.developmentCue: Move only when the body, timing, and path align.
nodes.masteryMarker: You can let life point the way without surrendering your center.
